facts.text: Herkimer village sits where West Canada Creek meets the Mohawk River, a historic portage spot used by early settlers.
The village grew around the Herkimer Home, a state historical site that preserves the farmstead of Revolutionary War hero Nicholas Herkimer.
In 1906, a sensational murder trial of Chester Gillette drew crowds to the county courthouse in Herkimer village, making headlines nationwide.
The town was first settled by German Palatines from the lower Rhine, bringing a distinct cultural heritage that still echoes in local traditions.
Herkimer County Community College, located in the village, offers programs that support regional workforce development and community enrichment.
The name “Herkimer” was originally intended for the territory south of the river, but an error led to the town and village adopting the name instead.
